metadata.title: Operation Ghost Energy Sector Attack Scenario
metadata.author: Cybersecurity Analyst

Scenario.overview: Energy Simple Operation Ghost targets the energy sector by exploiting vulnerabilities in internet-facing assets and executing malicious scripts to disrupt operations.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets Domain Name Registrar Network Traffic Command
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method PowerShell Script Network Traffic Content (Mechanism) Command Execution (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: An attacker sends a spear phishing email using compromised credentials from an employee's email account to gain access to the energy company's network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attacker uses PowerShell Empire to establish a command and control channel for lateral movement within the energy company's network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attacker uses WMI to execute malicious code on remote systems and maintain persistence within the energy company's network.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-24: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: The attacker exfiltrates sensitive data related to the energy company's operations, including grid management and customer information.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker launches a DoS attack on the energy company's critical infrastructure, causing a temporary disruption to power distribution and impacting customers.

Scenario.summary: Operation Ghost is a sophisticated attack targeting the energy sector, utilizing spear phishing emails to gain initial access, followed by execution of malicious scripts to disrupt operations and exfiltrate sensitive data.
